intro: The clearest difference between Tink and Veriff here is transparency: Tink scores 2/5 on our transparency rating, Veriff scores 3/5. Tink is European open banking platform providing bank account aggregation, payment initiation, and income verification APIs; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Veriff is Identity verification platform with automated document authentication, selfie matching, and fraud signal detection; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Pick Tink for Fintech, Marketplace; pick Veriff for Fintech, Marketplace. Tink is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Veriff is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Honest trade-offs: Tink is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements, and Veriff is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the KYC / KYB / AML API umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate. On pricing, Tink uses subscription pricing and Veriff uses subscription pricing starting around $49/ month, which materially changes total cost at scale.
quick_take: Tink is for fintech, marketplace; Veriff is for fintech, marketplace; decide based on scope fit.
verdict_a: Choose Tink if transparency rated 2/5 is acceptable and your use case is fintech, marketplace.
verdict_b: Choose Veriff if transparency rated 3/5 fits your requirements and your use case is fintech, marketplace.

faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Tink uses subscription pricing with no free tier published. Veriff uses subscription pricing starting at $49/ month.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Tink to Veriff?
faq.a: Migration is workable but not trivial, since both sit around medium lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data. Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Veriff a good alternative to Tink?
faq.a: Veriff sits in the same category as Tink, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Veriff is best fit for fintech, marketplace. Note: Veriff is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements.
